title: Skateboarder Spins into History
story: Skateboarding fans were amazed when Gui Khury pulled off an incredible trick. The 11-year-old Brazilian skateboarder spun his board three full rotations - a jaw-dropping 1080-degree turn - while soaring above a vertical ramp. This mind-bending feat on May 8, 2020, set a world record as the first-ever 1080 on a vertical ramp. Khury's gravity-defying spin showcased the exciting possibilities in skateboarding.

title: A Miraculous Escape from Clifton Bridge
story: Sarah Ann Henley's skirt became an unlikely lifesaver in 1885. Heartbroken, she leaped from the towering Clifton Bridge in Bristol, England on May 8. As she plummeted, her billowing crinoline skirt caught the wind, slowing her descent like a parachute. Amazingly, Sarah survived the fall into the River Avon below. Her incredible story of survival reminds us that life is precious and worth living, even in our darkest moments.

title: Monster Wave Crashes into Record Books

title: Buried Treasure: The Sutton Hoo Discovery
story: Shovels hit the ground as archaeologist Basil Brown began digging at Sutton Hoo in Suffolk. Little did he know, his work on May 8, 1939, would unearth an incredible treasure. Brown's team discovered a royal Anglo-Saxon burial ship from the 7th century, complete with priceless artifacts. This find, often called the greatest archaeological discovery on British soil, revealed fascinating secrets about life in ancient England.

title: Ancient Astrolabe Discovered in Shipwreck
story: Treasure hunters made an incredible find on May 8, 2014! They discovered the world's oldest astrolabe near Al Hallaniyah Island, Oman. This ancient navigation tool, used by sailors to find their way at sea, came from a Portuguese shipwreck. The astrolabe dates back to around 1498 and belonged to the famous explorer Vasco da Gama's fleet. It's an amazing piece of history that shows how brave sailors navigated the oceans over 500 years ago!

story: Batter up! On May 8, 1901, a future baseball star entered the world. Turkey Stearnes was born in Nashville, Tennessee, destined for greatness on the diamond. Stearnes swung his way to fame, earning All-Star honors and batting titles in the Negro Leagues.

title: The Man Behind Famous Movie Logos
story: Saul Bass, a creative genius, was born in New York City on May 8, 1920. He grew up to become a legendary graphic designer, crafting iconic logos and eye-catching movie title sequences. title: Jazz Genius Born: Keith Jarrett's Musical Journey
story: Fingers dancing across piano keys, Keith Jarrett began his musical journey on May 8, 1945.
